name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
                
      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production
      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}

          channelId: live

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, replaces a single occurrence, but can replace multiple occurrences when `expected_replacements` is specified. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the single instance to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations, or does not match exactly, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `expected_replacements` to the number of occurrences you want to replace. The tool will replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly. Ensure the number of replacements matches your expectation.

toolCalls.name: replace

args.new_string:
name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
                
      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production
      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

      - name: Install dependencies

      - name: Build

        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, replaces a single occurrence, but can replace multiple occurrences when `expected_replacements` is specified. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the single instance to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations, or does not match exactly, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `expected_replacements` to the number of occurrences you want to replace. The tool will replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly. Ensure the number of replacements matches your expectation.

toolCalls.name: replace

name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
                
      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production
      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

          ' > .env

          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"

        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, replaces a single occurrence, but can replace multiple occurrences when `expected_replacements` is specified. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the single instance to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations, or does not match exactly, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `expected_replacements` to the number of occurrences you want to replace. The tool will replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly. Ensure the number of replacements matches your expectation.

      - name: Create .env for Staging
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
                
      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production
      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the single instance to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations, or does not match exactly, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `expected_replacements` to the number of occurrences you want to replace. The tool will replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly. Ensure the number of replacements matches your expectation.

args.new_string:
name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
                
      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production
      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
                
      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production
      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
                
      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production
      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, replaces a single occurrence, but can replace multiple occurrences when `expected_replacements` is specified. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the single instance to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations, or does not match exactly, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `expected_replacements` to the number of occurrences you want to replace. The tool will replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly. Ensure the number of replacements matches your expectation.

messages.content: Okay, `deploy-preview` environment is back to `staging`. Next, I'll `replace` `FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}` with `FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }}`.

thoughts.subject: Revising Deployment Variables
thoughts.description: I've successfully updated the deployment environment name back to staging. My next step is to revise the Firebase configuration variable. I need to swap back from production secrets to staging secrets, which should be fairly straightforward.

thoughts.subject: Executing Variable Replacements
thoughts.description: I'm now focused on the variable replacement within the deployment configuration. Specifically, I'm using a `replace` function to swap the Firebase configuration from production to staging, changing the secret references. This should quickly align the deploy preview with the staging environment.

toolCalls.name: replace

args.new_string:
name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
                
      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production
      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

args.old_string:
name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
                
      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production
      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Generated .env content:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Staging
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_STAGING }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
                
      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production
      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging

          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel

          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}

          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '

          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production

        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production

          npm ci

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live
