resultDisplay.originalContent:
name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: production # Reflects the hosting target is production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging DB (Frontend config)
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }} # App connects to Staging DB
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging DB..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
 
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
            ' > .env

            STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
            echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV # Ensure GITHUB_ENV has the staging project ID for subsequent steps
            echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env

            echo "Generated .env content:"
            cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production Hosting
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json # Credentials for deploying to production hosting
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging Frontend
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging # Build frontend for staging DB

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}
    
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: production # Reflects the hosting target is production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging DB (Frontend config)
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }} # App connects to Staging DB
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging DB..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(JSON.parse(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
 
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
            ' > .env

            STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
            echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV # Ensure GITHUB_ENV has the staging project ID for subsequent steps
            echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env

            echo "Generated .env content:"
            cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production Hosting
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json # Credentials for deploying to production hosting
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging Frontend
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging # Build frontend for staging DB

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}
    
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(JSON.parse(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

messages.content:
Cette ligne, `JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")`, est une commande `bash` exécutant un script Node.js.

Elle vise à :
1.  **Lire** la configuration Firebase (attendue sous forme de chaîne d'objet JavaScript) depuis la variable d'environnement `FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON`.
2.  **L'évaluer** (`eval`) comme du code JavaScript pour la transformer en objet JavaScript. Le wrapping `(' + ... + ')` est là pour aider `eval` à interpréter la chaîne.
3.  **Convertir** cet objet JavaScript en une chaîne JSON standardisée (`JSON.stringify`).
4.  **Stocker** cette chaîne JSON résultante dans la variable shell `JSON_DATA`.
5.  **Gérer les erreurs** : si l'évaluation échoue (par exemple, si la chaîne n'est pas un objet JavaScript valide ou est vide), elle affiche une erreur et fait échouer l'étape GitHub Actions.

Les échecs précédents sont survenus car `FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON` contenait probablement une chaîne JSON stricte (ou était vide/mal formée), que `eval` n'a pas pu analyser correctement, provoquant des erreurs comme "Unexpected token ')'" ou "Unexpected end of JSON input". Utiliser `JSON.parse` est la méthode plus appropriée et plus sûre si le secret est déjà une chaîne JSON.

messages.content:
Okay, you're asking for an example of a JavaScript object string. It's essentially a string representation of a JavaScript object literal.

Here's an example of a Firebase configuration as a JavaScript object literal string:

```javascript
'{ apiKey: "VOTRE_API_KEY", authDomain: "votre-projet.firebaseapp.com", projectId: "votre-projet-id", storageBucket: "votre-projet.appspot.com", messagingSenderId: "VOTRE_SENDER_ID", appId: "VOTRE_APP_ID" }'
```

Notez que dans un littéral d'objet JavaScript, les clés n'ont pas nécessairement besoin d'être entre guillemets si ce sont des identifiants JavaScript valides. Cependant, les valeurs de type chaîne de caractères doivent toujours être entre guillemets simples ou doubles. Cela contraste avec le format JSON strict, où toutes les clés *doivent* être entre guillemets doubles.

args.content:
I understand your observation about line 41. However, the CI/CD logs clearly indicate that the `eval(...)` part is the cause of the parsing errors (`Unexpected token ')'` or `Unexpected end of JSON input`).

The function `eval()` expects a JavaScript object literal string. If `VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING` contains a standard JSON string (where keys are double-quoted, e.g., `{"key": "value"}`) or is empty, `eval()` will fail to parse it correctly.

The correct and robust way to parse a JSON string is `JSON.parse()`.

So, the problematic part `eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')')`
**MUST be changed to**
`JSON.parse(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON)`

This change needs to be applied in **both** the `Create .env for Staging DB (Frontend config)` step and the `Create .env for Production` step in `ci-cd.yml`.

Please make this specific change in both locations. Also, ensure that `VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING` and `VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION` secrets in GitHub are indeed valid **JSON strings** (with double quotes for keys).

Let me know once you have applied this fix and verified your secrets.

response.output:
name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging DB (Frontend config)
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }} # App connects to Staging DB
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging DB..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
 
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
            ' > .env

            STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
            echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV # Ensure GITHUB_ENV has the staging project ID for subsequent steps
            echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env

            echo "Generated .env content:"
            cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production Hosting
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json # Credentials for deploying to production hosting
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging Frontend
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging # Build frontend for staging DB

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}
    
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging DB (Frontend config)
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }} # App connects to Staging DB
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging DB..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
 
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
            ' > .env

            STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
            echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV # Ensure GITHUB_ENV has the staging project ID for subsequent steps
            echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env

            echo "Generated .env content:"
            cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production Hosting
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json # Credentials for deploying to production hosting
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging Frontend
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging # Build frontend for staging DB

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}
    
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging DB (Frontend config)
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }} # App connects to Staging DB
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging DB..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
 
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
            ' > .env

            STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
            echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV # Ensure GITHUB_ENV has the staging project ID for subsequent steps
            echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env

            echo "Generated .env content:"
            cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production Hosting
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json # Credentials for deploying to production hosting
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging Frontend
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging # Build frontend for staging DB

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}
    
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)

Replaces text within a file. By default, replaces a single occurrence, but can replace multiple occurrences when `expected_replacements` is specified. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the single instance to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations, or does not match exactly, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `expected_replacements` to the number of occurrences you want to replace. The tool will replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly. Ensure the number of replacements matches your expectation.

toolCalls.name: write_file

args.content:
I am still encountering difficulties with automatically applying the fix for the `eval` command in `ci-cd.yml` due to the strictness of the `replace` tool and the presence of identical lines in both the `deploy-preview` and `deploy-production` jobs.

The problematic line in both jobs is:
`JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")`

This line **needs to be changed to**:
`JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")`

The only change is the removal of the redundant `(` and `)` around `process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON` inside the `eval` call.

Could you please open your `.github/workflows/ci-cd.yml` file and manually make this specific change in **both** the `deploy-preview` job and the `deploy-production` job?

Let me know once you have made this manual change and saved the file. Then, we will proceed with committing the changes and triggering a new CI/CD run.

Thank you for providing the error. The message `Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: Invalid or unexpected token` confirms that the `eval()` method is failing to correctly interpret the secret content, even with JavaScript object notation.

The most reliable solution for this scenario in GitHub Actions is to ensure your secret is a **strict JSON string** and then use `JSON.parse()` to interpret it. This eliminates ambiguity and prevents unexpected token errors.

Please convert the content of your `VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING` (and `VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION` for consistency) secrets in GitHub to the following **strict JSON format** (all keys must be enclosed in double quotes):

response.output:
name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging DB (Frontend config)
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }} # App connects to Staging DB
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging DB..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
 
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
            ' > .env

            STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
            echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV # Ensure GITHUB_ENV has the staging project ID for subsequent steps
            echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env

            echo "Generated .env content:"
            cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production Hosting
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json # Credentials for deploying to production hosting
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging Frontend
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging # Build frontend for staging DB

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}
    
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          # Convert JS object literal to valid JSON using Node.js
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

toolCalls.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. If the file is large, the content will be truncated. The tool's response will clearly indicate if truncation has occurred and will provide details on how to read more of the file using the 'offset' and 'limit' parameters.         run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging # Build frontend for staging DB

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}
    
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging DB (Frontend config)
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }} # App connects to Staging DB
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging DB..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(JSON.parse(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
 
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
            ' > .env

            STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
            echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV # Ensure GITHUB_ENV has the staging project ID for subsequent steps
            echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env

            echo "Generated .env content:"
            cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production Hosting
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json # Credentials for deploying to production hosting
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging Frontend
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging # Build frontend for staging DB

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}
    
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(JSON.parse(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live

        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging Frontend
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging # Build frontend for staging DB

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}
    
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(JSON.parse(process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live
args.old_string:
name: CI/CD — Dashboard Mictlan (consolidated)

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (Vite)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build:production

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/

  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy Preview (PR)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    environment:
      name: staging
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Staging DB (Frontend config)
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_STAGING }} # App connects to Staging DB
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Generating .env file from standard Firebase config for Staging DB..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
 
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
            ' > .env

            STAGING_PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
            echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$STAGING_PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV # Ensure GITHUB_ENV has the staging project ID for subsequent steps
            echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env

            echo "Generated .env content:"
            cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production Hosting
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json # Credentials for deploying to production hosting
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Staging Frontend
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_GITHUB_PR_NUMBER=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode staging # Build frontend for staging DB

      - name: Compute preview channel id
        id: compute_channel
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER='${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}'
          if [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] || [ "$PR_NUMBER" = "null" ]; then
            CHANNEL="pr-${{ github.run_id }}"
          else
            CHANNEL="pr-$PR_NUMBER"
          fi
          echo "Using preview channel: $CHANNEL"
          echo "channel=$CHANNEL" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        shell: bash

      - name: Show dist contents (debug)
        run: |
          echo 'dist content:'
          ls -la dist || true

      - name: Deploy to Firebase preview channel
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: ${{ steps.compute_channel.outputs.channel }}
    
  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment:
      name: production
    permissions:
      checks: write
      pull-requests: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Use Node.js 20
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install jq
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y jq
        shell: bash

      - name: Create .env for Production
        env:
          FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON: ${{ secrets.VITE_FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON_PRODUCTION }}
          VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          echo "Setting up production environment..."
          JSON_DATA=$(node -e "try { console.log(JSON.stringify(eval('(' + process.env.FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON + ')'))); } catch (e) { console.error('Error parsing FIREBASE_CONFIG_JSON:', e.message); process.exit(1); }")
          
          echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '
            "VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY=\(.apiKey)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN=\(.authDomain)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=\(.projectId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=\(.storageBucket)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID=\(.messagingSenderId)",
            "VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID=\(.appId)"
          ' > .env

          PROJECT_ID=$(echo "$JSON_DATA" | jq -r '.projectId')
          echo "VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=$PROJECT_ID" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=${{ secrets.VITE_OPENWEATHER_API_KEY }}" >> .env
          
          echo "Environment variables set:"
          cat .env
        shell: bash

      - name: Create Google Credentials for Production
        run: echo '${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}' > google-credentials.json
        shell: bash

      - name: Set GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
        run: echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$(pwd)/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        shell: bash

      - name: Install dependencies & Build for Production
        run: |
          npm ci
          VITE_APP_BUILD_TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") npm run build -- --mode production

      - name: Deploy to Firebase (live)
        uses: FirebaseExtended/action-hosting-deploy@v0
        with:
          repoToken: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          firebaseServiceAccount: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRODUCTION }}
          projectId: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PROD_PROJECT_ID }}
          channelId: live
